headline: Target's new CEO says fresh eyes and respecting core values will help him regain customer trust
summary: Target plans to spend billions of dollars this year to end a persistent sales slump and to regain its image as a fun place to shop for stylish clothing, housewares, beauty products and packaged food at affordable prices.  The CEO guiding the turnaround plan is Michael Fiddelke, who stepped into the chief executive role last month but has worked for Target for more than 20 years.  Fiddelke, who previously served as chief operating officer, has his work cut out for him.
